sources.notes: Statement and constraints: find the shortest subarray whose maximum pairwise XOR is at least k; sum of n over tests is <= 2e5 and values are <= 1e9.
Luogu mirror of the statement was used to cross-check the definition of subarray beauty and constraints.
Official editorial code stores the latest index in each binary trie node and queries the nearest previous element y with x xor y >= k.
Maspy's short writeup confirms that only pairs involving the newly added endpoint need to be considered, and the predicate is handled by a binary trie.
Cnblogs explanation details the high-to-low trie traversal against threshold k and the O(n log A) complexity.

annotation.summary: Given an array a and threshold k, find the minimum length of a subarray whose maximum XOR over all pairs inside it is at least k, or report -1.
annotation.constraints: Up to 1e4 test cases, total n <= 2e5, 0 <= a_i,k <= 1e9. The solution needs roughly logarithmic work per element.
annotation.core_idea: A subarray is beautiful exactly when it contains two elements with XOR at least k; the two elements can serve as the endpoints of a shorter or equal beautiful subarray. Therefore, when scanning the right endpoint r, it is enough to find the largest previous index l such that a_l xor a_r >= k, then minimize r-l+1. Store all previous values in a binary trie, and in every trie node keep the maximum index among inserted numbers in that subtree. To query x against threshold k, scan bits from high to low. If k has bit 1, the XOR must also take bit 1 to remain able to meet the threshold while equal so far. If k has bit 0, taking XOR bit 1 already makes the value larger than k at this bit, so that opposite branch contributes all of its maximum index as a candidate; then continue through the equal branch with XOR bit 0. At the end, the equal branch is also valid. The largest valid index gives the shortest segment ending at r.
annotation.complexity: O(n log A) time and O(n log A) memory, with log A about 30
tricks: Reduce shortest beautiful subarray to choosing the closest pair of positions with XOR at least k.
Keep the maximum index in each binary trie subtree so a threshold query returns the nearest valid left endpoint.
Compare x xor y with k bit by bit: a 0-bit in k allows the opposite branch to be accepted immediately, while a 1-bit forces the opposite branch to stay equal.

solution_variants.name: latest-index-binary-trie
solution_variants.summary: Scan r from left to right. Before or while inserting a_r, query a binary trie containing seen values for the largest index l with a_l xor a_r >= k, using max-index values stored in trie subtrees.

tags.evidence: The main data structure is a 0-1 binary trie over value bits, with each node storing the latest inserted index in its subtree.
The query greedily processes bits from high to low according to whether the current bit of k is 0 or 1.
The condition x xor y >= k is decomposed by the first bit where the XOR value exceeds k, allowing whole trie subtrees to become candidates.
All comparisons and trie transitions are based on the binary representation of the values and k.
